name: CashMax Title & Loan
description_short: CashMax offers payday loans, cash advances, and title loans at 35+ Texas locations, with most loans funded in as little as 15 minutes.
description_long:
CashMax Title & Loan is a Texas-based short-term lending chain that has operated for over 20 years, building a regional presence across the state with more than 35 physical store locations. The Fort Worth location on NE 28th Street is one of many storefronts serving customers who need access to quick cash for unexpected expenses. The company positions itself as a community-oriented alternative to banks, emphasizing approachability and speed over formality.

CashMax offers three core products: payday loans, cash advances, and title loans. Payday loans and cash advances are small-dollar, short-term products typically tied to a borrower's next paycheck. Title loans use the borrower's vehicle as collateral, allowing for potentially larger loan amounts without traditional credit requirements. Applications can be submitted in-store, by phone, or online, and the company advertises that most loans can be completed in as little as 15 minutes.

What distinguishes CashMax within the payday and title loan space is its emphasis on a low-pressure customer experience. The company explicitly advertises no pushy or in-your-face sales tactics and highlights friendly, helpful staff as a core value. The Fort Worth location maintains extended Friday hours until 7pm and Saturday hours from 9am to 2pm, offering some flexibility for working borrowers. Customer testimonials on the location page point to repeat usage, staff responsiveness to payment timing, and overall positive service interactions.

Honestly, CashMax operates in a lending segment that carries significant financial risk for borrowers. Payday loans and title loans in Texas are regulated but can carry extremely high annualized interest rates — often several hundred percent APR. The website provides no rate, fee, or APR disclosures, making it impossible to evaluate cost before applying. Title loans specifically carry the risk of vehicle repossession if the borrower defaults. CashMax is a legitimate, long-standing regional lender, but these products should be treated as a last resort for genuine financial emergencies, not routine cash flow management.
pros: Most loans completed in as little as 15 minutes in-store
Over 35 Texas locations with a consistent regional presence
More than 20 years of operating history in Texas
Extended Friday hours (until 7pm) and Saturday availability (9am–2pm)
Multiple application channels: in-store, phone, and online
Offers both unsecured (payday/cash advance) and secured (title) loan options
cons: No APR, fee, or rate information disclosed anywhere on the website
Payday and title loans in Texas typically carry triple-digit APRs
Title loans risk vehicle repossession upon default
Sunday hours are unavailable; Saturday closes at 2pm
Operations limited to Texas — no service for out-of-state borrowers
best_for: Texas residents facing a genuine short-term financial emergency
Vehicle owners who need fast cash using their car title as collateral
Borrowers who prefer in-person service over online-only lenders
People who have exhausted lower-cost options and need same-day funding
diagnosis: CashMax is best suited for Texas residents who need small-dollar emergency cash quickly and have exhausted lower-cost options — particularly those who can use a vehicle title as collateral for a larger amount. The main caveat is that no rate or fee information is disclosed upfront, and payday and title loans in Texas routinely carry very high APRs, making these products expensive if not repaid on the original due date.
